question_title: Will the interest in “haiti” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?
summary: The question compares "haiti" Google Trends interest on Apr 11 vs Mar 30, with the start value at 44 (post-spike, after a 100 peak on Mar 29). The pre-spike baseline averaged ~48 with std ~5.5. Since the start value (44) sits below the baseline mean and the "Increases" threshold (>47) is very close to the baseline mean, mean reversion favors Increases. Multiple ongoing catalysts support sustained interest: the TPS House discharge petition succeeded (vote expected in weeks), GSF security transition starts April 1, Haiti plays Iceland March 31, and World Cup preparation continues. Monte Carlo simulations across 3 methods consistently favor Increases (~54%). The "Doesn't change" band [41-47] is relatively narrow (7 points) vs daily volatility (~5.5), and Decreases (<41) requires falling to the pre-spike trough level. I weight Increases highest but leave meaningful probability on DC and Decreases to account for uncertainty about whether 44 is the new baseline and measurement differences between SerpAPI and pytrends.

factors.description: Start value (44) is below pre-spike baseline mean (~48), so mean reversion favors Increases. The threshold for Increases (>47) is near the baseline mean.
Active ongoing news catalysts: TPS House vote expected in coming weeks, GSF security transition April 1, Haiti World Cup prep with friendly vs Iceland Mar 31. These maintain elevated interest.
Monte Carlo ensemble (3 methods) averages: Increases 54%, DC 29%, Decreases 17%. All methods favor Increases due to starting below baseline.
The DC band [41, 47] is only 7 points wide in a distribution with std ~5.5, making it relatively narrow and harder to land in over a 12-day horizon.
Pre-spike declining trend (56->41 over ~10 days) suggests interest was fading before the spike. If this trend resumes, values could drop below 41.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty of ±2 points. At boundaries (47 for Increases, 41 for Decreases), this uncertainty could shift outcomes.
Empirical day-to-day base rates show 50% no-change rate, but these are for consecutive day transitions, not the 12-day gap in this question. Over longer periods, drift accumulates.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Increases (50%), Doesn't change (30%), Decreases (20%)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **Start value of 44 is below baseline.** The pre-spike average for "haiti" in this window was ~48. Since the "Increases" threshold (>47) is very close to the baseline mean, simple mean reversion pushes the Apr 11 value into Increases territory.

- **The Mar 29 spike (100) was event-driven and has already collapsed.** The spike was caused by converging events: Haiti vs Tunisia match, TPS discharge petition reaching 218 signatures, a gang massacre, and Mitch Albom coverage. By Mar 30, interest dropped to 44.

- **Active catalysts in the forecast window.** The TPS House floor vote is imminent, the UN Gang Suppression Force transition starts April 1, Haiti plays Iceland on Mar 31, and World Cup interest is building. These support sustained or elevated interest.

- **Monte Carlo simulations favor Increases (~54%)** across three different modeling approaches, all consistent with the qualitative analysis.

- **The DC band [41-47] is narrow** relative to daily volatility (std ~5.5), making it a somewhat unlikely 12-day landing zone compared to the broader Increases range (48+).
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “haiti” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-11 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42964)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=haiti&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=haiti&date=2026-03-12%202026-04-11) for 2026-04-11 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-30. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "haiti",
  "date": "2026-03-12 2026-04-11",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
